checks.a_analysis: The output sections accurately reflect the headings, tables, and lists present in the source PDF. AI-generated metadata like the document title and image alt text are within acceptable parameters. No invented body text or sections were identified.

I verified phone numbers and URLs present in the output against the source. No fabricated contact information was found. While an email address is missing from the output, this is an omission of existing data rather than a fabrication of non-existent data.

The source PDF contains 38 pages. The output JSON explicitly states that 28 sections were omitted. A comparison reveals that major portions of the document, including the 10-page 'RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview' (Pages 7-17), the 'Saving Lives Together' marketing brochure (Pages 18-33), and sections on D-RAK introduction (Page 34) and payment options (Page 36), are missing entirely from the converted result. This represents approximately 73% of the source content.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains content on pages 7-27, 29-34, and 36, totaling approximately 28 pages of technical, marketing, and instructional material.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output JSON explicitly includes a placeholder: '_omitted': '28 sections omitted for token budget'. These sections are absent from the structured content.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically the gap between reading_order 36 and 355.

checks.a_analysis: The major tables in the document (Agenda on Page 1, Attendance on Page 3, Vote Records on Pages 4 and 6, and the Comparison Table on Page 28) were checked. In all cases, the row counts in the output match the data rows in the source PDF exactly.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Within the sections that were successfully converted, there are two notable text omissions. First, in Item 8 of the minutes (Page 4), the text is truncated mid-sentence ('...at the current co'), losing the words 'urse list for the'. Second, in the contact information section for Dan Jewiss (Page 35), the email address 'D.Jewiss@me.com' is entirely missing, leaving a non-grammatical sentence fragment ('Please contact Dan Jewiss at or 203-704-0581'). These omissions degrade the informational integrity of those specific sections.
checks.b_evidence_source:
1. '...approve the 911 4 Heroes Telecommunicator Training Bundle Online subscription at the current course list for the' (Page 4)
2. 'Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581' (Page 35)
checks.b_evidence_output:
1. '...approve the 911 4 Heroes Telecommunicator Training Bundle Online subscription at the current co' (reading_order 26)
2. 'Please contact Dan Jewiss at ' [followed by] ' or 203-704-0581' (reading_order 467 and 469)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Apart from the omissions noted in C5, the text that was converted appears to follow the source accurately. Minor reformatting such as bolding/italic adjustments or concatenation of address lines in the minutes header box are acceptable adjustments for accessibility and standardization.

checks.a_analysis: The output document was reviewed for instances of content duplication. Text that appears repeated, such as names in voting tables (e.g., 'JD Hartman' on page 6), accurately reflects intentional repetition found in the source document. No unintended duplication of paragraphs or sections was identified.

The heading hierarchy is generally logical and follows standard accessibility practices (e.g., using H1 for document titles and H2 for major sections). While there is an instance where an H2 precedes an H1 (elements 355-356), this falls under acceptable adjustments for accessibility normalization. No significant skips (e.g., H1 to H4) were found.

Two significant table structural errors were found. 1. In the Agenda table (page 1, element 7), the bulleted sub-items for agenda item 8 are removed from their associated table cell and placed into a separate list (element 8) after the entire table concludes. 2. In the Minutes vote table (page 6, element 35), the second row containing column headers is mislabeled as a data row, and the table title row is not configured with the correct column span.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on page 1, Tab 8 topic includes bullet points: '• Buncombe County: RQI Telecommunicator CPR Program...'; Vote table on page 6 has headers 'Committee Member', 'Vote to Approve', 'Vote to Not Approve'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 7 table excludes item 8 bullets; Element 35 table mislabels row 2 cell_type as 'data'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Agenda table (page 1, element 7); Vote table (page 6, element 35).

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is materially disrupted in two places. 1. On page 1, agenda item 8's sub-bullets are moved to the end of the agenda (after item 13), separating them from their parent topic. 2. On page 4 and 5, minutes item 8 is split between a heading element (26) and a paragraph element (28), and the split resulted in the truncation/loss of text ('urse list for the').
checks.b_evidence_source: Agenda item 8 sub-bullets appear before item 9; Minutes item 8 text reads: '...at the current course list for the subscription at 90%.'
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 8 (bullets) follows Element 7 (entire table 1-13); Element 26 ends with '...current co' and Element 28 begins with 'subscription at 90%.'

checks.a_analysis: Lists are identified correctly. Although some numbered lists in the source are converted to unordered lists in the output, the numeric labels are preserved within the text, maintaining the original meaning and sequence. No nesting errors were identified.

The output fails to include critical contact information present in the source. In the RAK Academy section, both the email address 'D.Jewiss@me.com' and the website URL 'www.TheRAKAcademy.com' are missing from the text.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text 'Please contact Dan Jewiss at or 203-704-0581...' is missing the contact email and website references.
